[May 28, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• US-Iran ceasefire/nuclear talks in flux — Axios said a 60-day MOU was reached but still needs Trump approval; later Iranian outlets denied finalization as strikes/tanker alerts hit Hormuz
• US data cools growth, keeps inflation sticky — Q1 GDP revised to 1.6% and April PCE matched core expectations, while claims ticked up and durable goods surprised higher
• Oil whipsaws on Iran headlines — crude, gas and gold swung sharply as ceasefire hopes, sanctions talk and new shipping/security risks moved markets
• S&P 500, Nasdaq hit records — despite geopolitical noise, US equities finished higher with broad risk appetite and mega-cap support

⛽ Oil & Energy
• EIA: US crude, gasoline, distillate stocks all fell — draws were larger than expected, supporting refined product tightness
• Bessent: Hormuz must stay open — US kept pressure on Iran, warned against tolls, and said oil should fall below pre-conflict levels if flows normalize
• Iranian shipping tensions persist — reports of attacks/fire exchanges near Hormuz kept energy risk premium alive
• Kazakhstan/Russia oil routing plans — reports pointed to more flows via BTC/China, while Russia eyed a lower oil budget floor

📊 Markets & Macro
• Fed split turns more hawkish on inflation risk — Williams and Musalem stressed data dependence, sticky near-term inflation, and less room for an easing bias
• ECB accounts flagged higher inflation risks — officials saw energy shocks as persistent, with little evidence yet of strong second-round effects
• Treasury auctions/market plumbing steady — 4-week and 7-year sales were absorbed fine; money-market assets rose to $7.78T

🌍 Geopolitical
• Middle East security worsens — Israel struck Beirut; Kuwait, Iran and regional officials issued hardline statements amid wider escalation risk
• Ukraine support continues — Sweden pledged $2.7B in military aid and Zelenskyy confirmed 150 jets
• Europe/Russia friction stays high — Russia pushed back on GPS-jamming claims and US troop moves to Poland; EU debated more strategic autonomy

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Anthropic price-tag shocks AI market — $65B Series H valued it at $965B, reinforcing the AI capex arms race
• CBOE wins SEC approval for extended-hours options — 23x5-style access expands, starting with ~20 large-cap names
• Bitcoin/crypto funding stay active — FalconX filed confidentially; Grayscale delayed IPO plans; new BNB ETF launch added product breadth

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• US/CFTC and FAA tighten oversight — CFTC sued to protect federal jurisdiction; FAA imposed no-drone zones for 2026 World Cup sites
• EU pushes chip and capital markets support — Brussels sought crisis powers over chip supply and more funding for local chip production
• US trade/tech policy intensifies — Bessent backed a crypto clarity act and said there will be no CBDC; USTR warned on streaming quotas

🏢 Corporate
• Dell and Snowflake post standout results — Dell blew past Q1 estimates and guided higher; Snowflake surged on strong earnings and AWS expansion
• Best Buy tops expectations, Kohl’s still pressured — BBY beat on sales/EPS; KSS beat but remains weak with cautious outlook
• MSFT/AI and cloud momentum — Microsoft plans new coding and task-specialized models; Amazon may offer xAI’s Grok on its AI platform
• Defense, consumer and industrial names get support — Lockheed/Northrop outlooks improved, Costco beat estimates, and Southwest cited resilient travel demand
